# Burrow

> Burrow is a modular Go web framework for server-rendered web applications. Built on Chi, Bun/SQLite, and Go's html/template. Deploy as a single binary.

Burrow follows the Django/Rails philosophy: composable apps with their own routes, migrations, middleware, and templates â€” but with Go's performance and single-binary deployment. It uses embedded SQLite (pure Go, no CGO) so applications need zero external dependencies at runtime.

Burrow is designed for server-rendered web applications, not API-only services.

## Core Concepts

- [Quick Start](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/getting-started/quickstart/): Minimal app setup
- [Creating an App](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/guide/creating-an-app/): Build a custom app with routes, templates, and migrations
- [Core Interfaces](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/reference/interfaces/): App, Migratable, HasRoutes, HasMiddleware, HasTemplates, Configurable, and more
- [Core Functions](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/reference/core-functions/): HandlerFunc, Handle, RenderTemplate, Bind, Validate, JSON, Text, HTML
- [Routing](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/guide/routing/): Handlers, error handling, URL parameters, middleware
- [Database](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/guide/database/): SQLite setup, PRAGMAs, Bun ORM usage, repository pattern
- [Layouts & Rendering](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/guide/layouts/): Template rendering, layout wrapping, HTMX support
- [Configuration](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/guide/configuration/): CLI flags, environment variables, TOML config
- [Migrations](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/guide/migrations/): Per-app SQL migrations with tracking
- [Internationalization](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/guide/i18n/): Built-in i18n with Accept-Language detection and go-i18n translations

## Contrib Apps

- [Auth](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/contrib/auth/): WebAuthn/passkey authentication with recovery codes
- [Session](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/contrib/session/): Cookie-based sessions via gorilla/sessions
- [CSRF](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/contrib/csrf/): CSRF protection via gorilla/csrf
- [Admin](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/contrib/admin/): Admin panel coordinator with ModelAdmin CRUD
- [Bootstrap](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/contrib/bootstrap/): Bootstrap 5 CSS/JS, dark mode, layout
- [Alpine.js](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/contrib/alpine/): Alpine.js asset serving with content-hashed URLs
- [htmx](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/contrib/htmx/): HTMX integration with request/response helpers
- [Jobs](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/contrib/jobs/): SQLite-backed background job queue
- [Uploads](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/contrib/uploads/): File upload storage with content-hashed serving
- [Messages](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/contrib/messages/): Flash messages
- [Rate Limiting](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/contrib/ratelimit/): Per-client token bucket rate limiting
- [Static Files](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/contrib/staticfiles/): Content-hashed static file serving
- [Healthcheck](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/contrib/healthcheck/): Liveness and readiness probes

## Reference

- [Server & Registry](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/reference/server/): Server boot sequence, registry API
- [Configuration Reference](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/reference/configuration/): All flags, env vars, TOML keys
- [Template Functions](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/reference/template-functions/): Built-in template functions
- [Context Helpers](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/reference/context-helpers/): Request context accessors

## Quick Conventions for Coding Agents

These are the non-obvious patterns and rules that matter most when generating Burrow code.

### Handler Pattern

Handlers return `error` â€” never call `http.Error()` directly:

```go
r.Get("/notes", burrow.Handle(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) error {
    notes, err := repo.List(r.Context())
    if err != nil {
        return err // 500, logged automatically
    }
    return burrow.Render(w, r, http.StatusOK, "notes/list", notes)
}))
```

Return `burrow.NewHTTPError(code, msg)` for client errors (404, 403, etc.). Any other error becomes a 500.

### App Structure

Every app lives in its own package and implements `burrow.App`:

```go
func (a *myApp) Name() string          { return "notes" }
func (a *myApp) Register(cfg *burrow.AppConfig) error { /* init repos, services */ }
```

Optional interfaces add capabilities: `HasRoutes`, `HasMiddleware`, `HasTemplates`, `Migratable`, `Configurable`, `HasFuncMap`, `HasRequestFuncMap`, `HasNavItems`, `HasAdmin`, `HasTranslations`, `HasDependencies`, `HasJobs`, `HasStaticFiles`, `HasShutdown`.

File layout: `app.go`, `context.go`, `handlers.go`, `middleware.go`, `models.go`, `repository.go`, `templates/*.html`, `migrations/*.sql`.

### Models & Database

SQLite via Bun ORM â€” pure Go, no CGO required:

```go
type Note struct {
    bun.BaseModel `bun:"table:notes,alias:n"`
    ID    int64  `bun:",pk,autoincrement"`
    Title string `bun:",notnull"`
}
```

Always set a table alias. Always qualify column names with `?TableAlias` in queries that use `.Relation()` JOINs:

```go
q.Where("?TableAlias.user_id = ?", userID)
```

### Migrations

Embedded SQL files per app, tracked in `_migrations` table:

```go
//go:embed migrations
var migrationFS embed.FS
```

Files are named `001_create_notes.up.sql`, `002_add_index.up.sql`, etc.

### Templates

All templates are parsed into a single global `*template.Template`. Namespace with app name:

```html
{{ define "notes/list" }}
<h1>Notes</h1>
{{ range .Items }}<p>{{ .Title }}</p>{{ end }}
{{ end }}
```

Embed template files in the app:

```go
//go:embed templates/*.html
var templateFS embed.FS
```

### Context Helpers

Each package owns its context accessors. Naming convention â€” getter: `X(ctx)`, setter: `WithX(ctx, val)`:

```go
user := auth.CurrentUser(ctx)
token := csrf.Token(ctx)
items := burrow.NavItems(ctx)
layout := burrow.Layout(ctx)
```

Context helpers never live in the root `burrow` package unless they are core framework concerns.

### Response Helpers

- `burrow.Render(w, r, code, templateName, data)` â€” render template with layout wrapping
- `burrow.JSON(w, code, v)` â€” JSON response
- `burrow.Text(w, code, s)` â€” plain text
- `burrow.HTML(w, code, s)` â€” raw HTML

### Request Binding & Validation

```go
var input CreateNoteInput
if err := burrow.Bind(r, &input); err != nil { return err }
if err := burrow.Validate(input); err != nil { return err }
```

Use `validate` struct tags. `*ValidationError` has `[]FieldError` and `HasField()`.

### Routing

Uses chi directly â€” `chi.URLParam(r, "id")` for path params. Middleware is stdlib `func(http.Handler) http.Handler`.

### Pagination

```go
pr := burrow.ParsePageRequest(r)
q := burrow.ApplyOffset(q, pr)
result := burrow.OffsetResult(pr, totalCount)
url := burrow.PageURL(basePath, r.URL.RawQuery, page) // preserves existing query params
```

## Optional

- [Tutorial](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/tutorial/): Build a complete polls app step by step (7 parts)
- [TLS](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/guide/tls/): TLS modes, ACME/Let's Encrypt, self-signed certs
- [Deployment](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/guide/deployment/): Production deployment guide
- [Contributing](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/contributing/): Development setup and contribution workflow
- [Changelog](https://burrow.someonewho.codes/changelog/): Version history
